  \       <property name=\"Authentication Strategy\">SIMPLE</property>\r\n        <property

  \       <property name=\"TLS - Truststore Password\">{{.Values.auth.SSL.truststorePasswd}}</property>\r\n

  \       <property name=\"User Search Base\">{{.Values.auth.ldap.searchBase}}</property>\r\n

  \       <property name=\"User Identity Attribute\">{{.Values.auth.ldap.UserIdentityAttribute}}</property>\r\n
  \       <property name=\"User Group Name Attribute\"></property>\r\n        <property
  name=\"User Group Name Attribute - Referenced Group Attribute\"></property>\r\n
  \       <property name=\"Group Search Base\"></property>\r\n        <property name=\"Group
  Object Class\">group</property>\r\n        <property name=\"Group Search Scope\">{{.Values.auth.ldap.groupSearchScope}}</property>\r\n
  \       <property name=\"Group Search Filter\"></property>\r\n        <property
  name=\"Group Name Attribute\"></property>\r\n        <property name=\"Group Member
  Attribute\"></property>\r\n        <property name=\"Group Member Attribute - Referenced

  \   -->\r\n    {{- if .Values.auth.ldap.enabled}}\r\n    <userGroupProvider>\r\n
  \       <identifier>composite-configurable-user-group-provider</identifier>\r\n
  \       <class>org.apache.nifi.authorization.CompositeConfigurableUserGroupProvider</class>\r\n

  \       <property name=\"User Group Provider 1\">ldap-user-group-provider</property>\r\n
  \   </userGroupProvider>\r\n    {{- end}}\r\n\r\n    <!--\r\n        The CompositeConfigurableUserGroupProvider
  will provide support for retrieving users and groups from multiple sources.\r\n
  \       Additionally, a single configurable user group provider is required. Users
  from the configurable user group provider\r\n        are configurable, however users
  loaded from one of the User Group Provider [unique key] will not be.\r\n        -
  Configurable User Group Provider - A configurable user group provider.\r\n        -
  User Group Provider [unique key] - The identifier of user group providers to load
  from. The name of\r\n            each property must be unique, for example: \"User
  Group Provider A\", \"User Group Provider B\",\r\n            \"User Group Provider
  C\" or \"User Group Provider 1\", \"User Group Provider 2\", \"User Group Provider
  3\"\r\n            NOTE: Any identity mapping rules specified in nifi.properties
  are not applied in this implementation. This behavior\r\n            would need
  to be applied by the base implementation.\r\n    -->\r\n    <!-- To enable the composite-configurable-user-group-provider
  remove 2 lines. This is 1 of 2.\r\n    <userGroupProvider>\r\n        <identifier>composite-configurable-user-group-provider</identifier>\r\n
  \       <class>org.apache.nifi.authorization.CompositeConfigurableUserGroupProvider</class>\r\n
  \       <property name=\"Configurable User Group Provider\">file-user-group-provider</property>\r\n
  \       <property name=\"User Group Provider 1\"></property>\r\n    </userGroupProvider>\r\n
  \   To enable the composite-configurable-user-group-provider remove 2 lines. This
  is 2 of 2. -->\r\n\r\n    <!--\r\n        The FileAccessPolicyProvider will provide
  support for managing access policies which is backed by a file\r\n        on the
  local file system.\r\n        - User Group Provider - The identifier for an User
  Group Provider defined above that will be used to access\r\n            users and
  groups for use in the managed access policies.\r\n        - Authorizations File
  - The file where the FileAccessPolicyProvider will store policies.\r\n        -
  Initial Admin Identity - The identity of an initial admin user that will be granted
  access to the UI and\r\n            given the ability to create additional users,
  groups, and policies. The value of this property could be\r\n            a DN when
  using certificates or LDAP, or a Kerberos principal. This property will only be
  used when there\r\n            are no other policies defined. If this property is
  specified then a Legacy Authorized Users File can not be specified.\r\n            NOTE:
  Any identity mapping rules specified in nifi.properties will also be applied to
  the initial admin identity,\r\n            so the value should be the unmapped identity.
  This identity must be found in the configured User Group Provider.\r\n        -
  Legacy Authorized Users File - The full path to an existing authorized-users.xml
  that will be automatically\r\n            converted to the new authorizations model.
  If this property is specified then an Initial Admin Identity can\r\n            not
  be specified, and this property will only be used when there are no other users,
  groups, and policies defined.\r\n            NOTE: Any users in the legacy users
  file must be found in the configured User Group Provider.\r\n        - Node Identity
  [unique key] - The identity of a NiFi cluster node. When clustered, a property for
  each node\r\n            should be defined, so that every node knows about every
  other node. If not clustered these properties can be ignored.\r\n            The
  name of each property must be unique, for example for a three node cluster:\r\n
  \           \"Node Identity A\", \"Node Identity B\", \"Node Identity C\" or \"Node
  Identity 1\", \"Node Identity 2\", \"Node Identity 3\"\r\n            NOTE: Any
  identity mapping rules specified in nifi.properties will also be applied to the
  node identities,\r\n            so the values should be the unmapped identities
  (i.e. full DN from a certificate). This identity must be found\r\n            in
  the configured User Group Provider.\r\n    -->\r\n    <accessPolicyProvider>\r\n
  \       <identifier>file-access-policy-provider</identifier>\r\n        <class>org.apache.nifi.authorization.FileAccessPolicyProvider</class>\r\n
  \       <property name=\"User Group Provider\">file-user-group-provider</property>\r\n
  \       <property name=\"Authorizations File\">./auth-conf/authorizations.xml</property>\r\n
  \       {{- if and .Values.auth.ldap.enabled (not .Values.auth.admin) }}\r\n        <property
  name=\"Initial Admin Identity\">{{.Values.auth.ldap.admin}}</property>\r\n        {{-
  else }}\r\n        <property name=\"Initial Admin Identity\">{{ .Values.auth.admin
  }}</property>\r\n        {{- end}}\r\n        <property name=\"Legacy Authorized
  Users File\"></property>\r\n        {{- range $i := until $replicas }}\r\n        <property
  name=\"Node Identity {{ $i }}\">CN={{ $fullname }}-{{ $i }}.{{ $fullname }}-headless.{{
  $namespace }}.svc.cluster.local, OU=NIFI</property>\r\n        {{- end }}\r\n        <property
  name=\"Node Identity\"></property>\r\n    </accessPolicyProvider>\r\n     <!--\r\n
  \       The StandardManagedAuthorizer. This authorizer implementation must be configured
  with the\r\n        Access Policy Provider which it will use to access and manage
  users, groups, and policies.\r\n        These users, groups, and policies will be
  used to make all access decisions during authorization\r\n        requests.\r\n
  \       - Access Policy Provider - The identifier for an Access Policy Provider
  defined above.\r\n    -->\r\n    <authorizer>\r\n        <identifier>managed-authorizer</identifier>\r\n
  \       <class>org.apache.nifi.authorization.StandardManagedAuthorizer</class>\r\n
  \       <property name=\"Access Policy Provider\">file-access-policy-provider</property>\r\n
  \   </authorizer>\r\n    <!--\r\n        NOTE: This Authorizer has been replaced
  with the more granular approach configured above with the Standard\r\n        Managed
  Authorizer. However, it is still available for backwards compatibility reasons.\r\n
  \       The FileAuthorizer is NiFi's provided authorizer and has the following properties:\r\n
  \       - Authorizations File - The file where the FileAuthorizer will store policies.\r\n
  \       - Users File - The file where the FileAuthorizer will store users and groups.\r\n
  \       - Initial Admin Identity - The identity of an initial admin user that will
  be granted access to the UI and\r\n            given the ability to create additional
  users, groups, and policies. The value of this property could be\r\n            a
  DN when using certificates or LDAP, or a Kerberos principal. This property will
  only be used when there\r\n            are no other users, groups, and policies
  defined. If this property is specified then a Legacy Authorized\r\n            Users
  File can not be specified.\r\n            NOTE: Any identity mapping rules specified
  in nifi.properties will also be applied to the initial admin identity,\r\n            so
  the value should be the unmapped identity.\r\n        - Legacy Authorized Users
  File - The full path to an existing authorized-users.xml that will be automatically\r\n
  \           converted to the new authorizations model. If this property is specified
  then an Initial Admin Identity can\r\n            not be specified, and this property
  will only be used when there are no other users, groups, and policies defined.\r\n
  \       - Node Identity [unique key] - The identity of a NiFi cluster node. When
  clustered, a property for each node\r\n            should be defined, so that every
  node knows about every other node. If not clustered these properties can be ignored.\r\n
  \           The name of each property must be unique, for example for a three node
  cluster:\r\n            \"Node Identity A\", \"Node Identity B\", \"Node Identity
  C\" or \"Node Identity 1\", \"Node Identity 2\", \"Node Identity 3\"\r\n            NOTE:
  Any identity mapping rules specified in nifi.properties will also be applied to
  the node identities,\r\n            so the values should be the unmapped identities
  (i.e. full DN from a certificate).\r\n    -->\r\n    {{- if .Values.auth.ldap.enabled}}\r\n
  \   <authorizer>\r\n        <identifier>file-provider</identifier>\r\n        <class>org.apache.nifi.authorization.FileAuthorizer</class>\r\n
  \       <property name=\"Authorizations File\">./auth-conf/authorizations.xml</property>\r\n
  \       <property name=\"Users File\">./auth-conf/users.xml</property>\r\n        <property
  name=\"Initial Admin Identity\">{{.Values.auth.ldap.admin}}</property>\r\n        <property
  name=\"Legacy Authorized Users File\"></property>\r\n\r\n    </authorizer>\r\n    {{-
  end}}\r\n</authorizers>\r\n"
bootstrap-notification-services.xml: "<?xml version=\"1.0\"?>\r\n<!--\r\n  Licensed

  on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\r\n  WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either
  express or implied.\r\n  See the License for the specific language governing permissions
  and\r\n  limitations under the License.\r\n-->\r\n<services>\r\n    <!-- This file
  is used to define how interested parties are notified when events in NiFi's lifecycle
  occur. -->\r\n    <!-- The format of this file is:\r\n        <services>\r\n            <service>\r\n
  \               <id>service-identifier</id>\r\n                <class>org.apache.nifi.notifications.DesiredNotificationService</class>\r\n
  \               <property name=\"property name\">property value</property>\r\n                <property
  name=\"another property\">another property value</property>\r\n            </service>\r\n
  \       </services>\r\n        \r\n        This file can contain 0 to many different
  service definitions.\r\n        The id can then be referenced from the bootstrap.conf
  file in order to configure the notification service\r\n        to be used when particular
  lifecycle events occur.\r\n    -->\r\n    \r\n<!--\r\n     <service>\r\n        <id>email-notification</id>\r\n
  \       <class>org.apache.nifi.bootstrap.notification.email.EmailNotificationService</class>\r\n
  \       <property name=\"SMTP Hostname\"></property>\r\n        <property name=\"SMTP
  Port\"></property>\r\n        <property name=\"SMTP Username\"></property>\r\n        <property
  name=\"SMTP Password\"></property>\r\n        <property name=\"SMTP TLS\"></property>\r\n
  \       <property name=\"From\"></property>\r\n        <property name=\"To\"></property>\r\n
  \    </service>\r\n-->\r\n<!--\r\n     <service>\r\n        <id>http-notification</id>\r\n
  \       <class>org.apache.nifi.bootstrap.notification.http.HttpNotificationService</class>\r\n
  \       <property name=\"URL\"></property>\r\n     </service>\r\n-->\r\n</services>\r\n"
bootstrap.conf: "#\r\n# Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one
  or more\r\n# contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with\r\n#
  this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\r\n# The ASF
  licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\r\n# (the \"License\");
  you may not use this file except in compliance with\r\n# the License.  You may obtain
  a copy of the License at\r\n#\r\n#   http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\r\n#\r\n#
  Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\r\n# distributed
  under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\r\n# WITHOUT WARRANTIES
  OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\r\n# See the License for the
  specific language governing permissions and\r\n# limitations under the License.\r\n#\r\n\r\n#
  Java command to use when running NiFi\r\njava=java\r\n\r\n# Username to use when
  running NiFi. This value will be ignored on Windows.\r\nrun.as=\r\n\r\n# Configure
  where NiFi's lib and conf directories live\r\nlib.dir=./lib\r\nconf.dir=./conf\r\n\r\n#
  How long to wait after telling NiFi to shutdown before explicitly killing the Process\r\ngraceful.shutdown.seconds=20\r\n\r\n#
  Disable JSR 199 so that we can use JSP's without running a JDK\r\njava.arg.1=-Dorg.apache.jasper.compiler.disablejsr199=true\r\n\r\n#
  JVM memory settings\r\njava.arg.2=-Xms{{.Values.jvmMemory}}\r\njava.arg.3=-Xmx{{.Values.jvmMemory}}\r\n\r\n#
  Enable Remote Debugging\r\n#java.arg.debug=-agentlib:jdwp=transport=dt_socket,server=y,suspend=n,address=8000\r\n\r\njava.arg.4=-Djava.net.preferIPv4Stack=true\r\n\r\n#
  allowRestrictedHeaders is required for Cluster/Node communications to work properly\r\njava.arg.5=-Dsun.net.http.allowRestrictedHeaders=true\r\njava.arg.6=-Djava.protocol.handler.pkgs=sun.net.www.protocol\r\n\r\n{{
  if .Values.certManager.replaceDefaultTrustStore }}\r\njava.arg.7=-Djavax.net.ssl.trustStore=/opt/nifi/nifi-current/tls/truststore.jks\r\n{{/*
  if .Values.certManager.replaceDefaultTrustStore */}}{{ end }}\r\n\r\n# The G1GC
  is still considered experimental but has proven to be very advantageous in providing
  great\r\n# performance without significant \"stop-the-world\" delays.\r\n#java.arg.13=-XX:+UseG1GC\r\n\r\n#Set
  headless mode by default\r\njava.arg.14=-Djava.awt.headless=true\r\n\r\n# Master
  key in hexadecimal format for encrypted sensitive configuration values\r\nnifi.bootstrap.sensitive.key=\r\n\r\n#
  Sets the provider of SecureRandom to /dev/urandom to prevent blocking on VMs\r\njava.arg.15=-Djava.security.egd=file:/dev/urandom\r\n\r\n###\r\n#
  Notification Services for notifying interested parties when NiFi is stopped, started,
  dies\r\n###\r\n\r\n# XML File that contains the definitions of the notification
  services\r\nnotification.services.file=./conf/bootstrap-notification-services.xml\r\n\r\n#
  In the case that we are unable to send a notification for an event, how many times
  should we retry?\r\nnotification.max.attempts=5\r\n\r\n# Comma-separated list of
  identifiers that are present in the notification.services.file; which services should
  be used to notify when NiFi is started?\r\n#nifi.start.notification.services=email-notification\r\n\r\n#
  Comma-separated list of identifiers that are present in the notification.services.file;
  which services should be used to notify when NiFi is stopped?\r\n#nifi.stop.notification.services=email-notification\r\n\r\n#
  Comma-separated list of identifiers that are present in the notification.services.file;
  which services should be used to notify when NiFi dies?\r\n#nifi.dead.notification.services=email-notification\r\n\r\n\r\n#
  Extra bootstrap options\r\n{{- range .Values.extraOptions }}\r\n{{ .name }}={{ .value
  }}\r\n{{- end }}\r\n\r\n"
flow.xml: "<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\" standalone=\"no\"?>\r\n<flowController
  encoding-version=\"1.3\">\r\n    <maxTimerDrivenThreadCount>10</maxTimerDrivenThreadCount>\r\n
  \   <maxEventDrivenThreadCount>5</maxEventDrivenThreadCount>\r\n    <registries>\r\n
  \       <flowRegistry>\r\n            <id>{{ default uuidv4 }}</id>\r\n            <name>default</name>\r\n
  \           <url>{{ template \"registry.url\" . }}</url>\r\n            <description/>\r\n
  \       </flowRegistry>\r\n    </registries>\r\n    <rootGroup>\r\n        <id>{{
  default uuidv4 }}</id>\r\n        <name>Nifi Flow</name>\r\n        <position x=\"0.0\"
  y=\"0.0\"/>\r\n        <comment/>\r\n    </rootGroup>\r\n    <controllerServices/>\r\n
  \   <reportingTasks/>\r\n</flowController>\r\n"
login-identity-providers-ldap.xml: "<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\" standalone=\"yes\"?>\r\n<!--\r\n
  \ Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\r\n  contributor
  license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with\r\n  this work for additional
  information regarding copyright ownership.\r\n  The ASF licenses this file to You
  under the Apache License, Version 2.0\r\n  (the \"License\"); you may not use this
  file except in compliance with\r\n  the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License
  at\r\n      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\r\n  Unless required by applicable
  law or agreed to in writing, software\r\n  distributed under the License is distributed
  on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\r\n  WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either
  express or implied.\r\n  See the License for the specific language governing permissions
  and\r\n  limitations under the License.\r\n-->\r\n<!--\r\n    This file lists the
  login identity providers to use when running securely. In order\r\n    to use a
  specific provider it must be configured here and it's identifier\r\n    must be
  specified in the nifi.properties file.\r\n-->\r\n<loginIdentityProviders>\r\n    <!--\r\n
  \       Identity Provider for users logging in with username/password against an
  LDAP server.\r\n        \r\n        'Authentication Strategy' - How the connection
  to the LDAP server is authenticated. Possible\r\n            values are ANONYMOUS,
  SIMPLE, LDAPS, or START_TLS.\r\n        \r\n        'Manager DN' - The DN of the
  manager that is used to bind to the LDAP server to search for users.\r\n        'Manager
  Password' - The password of the manager that is used to bind to the LDAP server
  to\r\n            search for users.\r\n            \r\n        'TLS - Keystore'
  - Path to the Keystore that is used when connecting to LDAP using LDAPS or START_TLS.\r\n
  \       'TLS - Keystore Password' - Password for the Keystore that is used when
  connecting to LDAP\r\n            using LDAPS or START_TLS.\r\n        'TLS - Keystore
  Type' - Type of the Keystore that is used when connecting to LDAP using\r\n            LDAPS
  or START_TLS (i.e. JKS or PKCS12).\r\n        'TLS - Truststore' - Path to the Truststore
  that is used when connecting to LDAP using LDAPS or START_TLS.\r\n        'TLS -
  Truststore Password' - Password for the Truststore that is used when connecting
  to\r\n            LDAP using LDAPS or START_TLS.\r\n        'TLS - Truststore Type'
  - Type of the Truststore that is used when connecting to LDAP using\r\n            LDAPS
  or START_TLS (i.e. JKS or PKCS12).\r\n        'TLS - Client Auth' - Client authentication
  policy when connecting to LDAP using LDAPS or START_TLS.\r\n            Possible
  values are REQUIRED, WANT, NONE.\r\n        'TLS - Protocol' - Protocol to use when
  connecting to LDAP using LDAPS or START_TLS. (i.e. TLS,\r\n            TLSv1.1,
  TLSv1.2, etc).\r\n        'TLS - Shutdown Gracefully' - Specifies whether the TLS
  should be shut down gracefully \r\n            before the target context is closed.
  Defaults to false.\r\n            \r\n        'Referral Strategy' - Strategy for
  handling referrals. Possible values are FOLLOW, IGNORE, THROW.\r\n        'Connect
  Timeout' - Duration of connect timeout. (i.e. 10 secs).\r\n        'Read Timeout'
  - Duration of read timeout. (i.e. 10 secs).\r\n       \r\n        'Url' - Space-separated
  list of URLs of the LDAP servers (i.e. ldap://<hostname>:<port>).\r\n        'User
  Search Base' - Base DN for searching for users (i.e. CN=Users,DC=example,DC=com).\r\n
  \       'User Search Filter' - Filter for searching for users against the 'User
  Search Base'.\r\n            (i.e. sAMAccountName={0}). The user specified name
  is inserted into '{0}'.\r\n        'Identity Strategy' - Strategy to identify users.
  Possible values are USE_DN and USE_USERNAME.\r\n            The default functionality
  if this property is missing is USE_DN in order to retain\r\n            backward
  compatibility. USE_DN will use the full DN of the user entry if possible.\r\n            USE_USERNAME
  will use the username the user logged in with.\r\n        'Authentication Expiration'
  - The duration of how long the user authentication is valid\r\n            for.
  If the user never logs out, they will be required to log back in following\r\n            this
  duration.\r\n    -->\r\n    <provider>\r\n        <identifier>ldap-provider</identifier>\r\n
  \       <class>org.apache.nifi.ldap.LdapProvider</class>\r\n        <property name=\"Authentication
  Strategy\">{{.Values.auth.ldap.authStrategy}}</property>\r\n        <property name=\"Manager
  DN\">{{.Values.auth.ldap.admin}}</property>\r\n        <property name=\"Manager
  Password\">{{.Values.auth.ldap.pass}}</property>\r\n        <property name=\"TLS
  - Keystore\">/opt/nifi/nifi-current/conf/{{.Release.Name}}-nifi-0.{{.Release.Name}}-nifi-headless.{{.Release.Namespace}}.svc.cluster.local/keystore.jks</property>\r\n
  \       <property name=\"TLS - Keystore Password\">{{.Values.auth.keystorePasswd}}</property>\r\n
  \       <property name=\"TLS - Keystore Type\">JKS</property>\r\n        <property
  name=\"TLS - Truststore\">/opt/nifi/nifi-current/conf/{{.Release.Name}}-nifi-0.{{.Release.Name}}-nifi-headless.{{.Release.Namespace}}.svc.cluster.local/truststore.jks</property>\r\n
  \       <property name=\"TLS - Truststore Password\">{{.Values.auth.truststorePasswd}}</property>\r\n
  \       <property name=\"TLS - Truststore Type\">JKS</property>\r\n        <property
  name=\"TLS - Client Auth\">NONE</property>\r\n        <property name=\"TLS - Protocol\">TLS</property>\r\n
  \       <property name=\"TLS - Shutdown Gracefully\">false</property>\r\n\r\n        <property
  name=\"Referral Strategy\">FOLLOW</property>\r\n        <property name=\"Connect
  Timeout\">10 secs</property>\r\n        <property name=\"Read Timeout\">10 secs</property>\r\n
  \       <property name=\"Url\">{{.Values.auth.ldap.host}}</property>\r\n        <property
  name=\"User Search Base\">{{.Values.auth.ldap.searchBase}}</property>\r\n        <property
  name=\"User Search Filter\">({{.Values.auth.ldap.userIdentityAttribute}}={0})</property>\r\n
  \       <property name=\"Identity Strategy\">{{.Values.auth.ldap.IdentityStrategy}}</property>\r\n
  \       <property name=\"Authentication Expiration\">{{.Values.auth.ldap.authExpiration}}</property>\r\n
  \   </provider>\r\n</loginIdentityProviders>\r\n"
nifi.properties: "# Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or
  more\r\n# contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with\r\n#
  this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\r\n# The ASF
  licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\r\n# (the \"License\");
  you may not use this file except in compliance with\r\n# the License.  You may obtain
  a copy of the License at\r\n#\r\n#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\r\n#\r\n#
  Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\r\n# distributed
  under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\r\n# WITHOUT WARRANTIES
  OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\r\n# See the License for the
  specific language governing permissions and\r\n# limitations under the License.\r\n\r\n#
  Core Properties #\r\nnifi.flow.configuration.file=../data/flow.xml.gz\r\nnifi.flow.configuration.archive.enabled=true\r\nnifi.flow.configuration.archive.dir=../data/archive/\r\nnifi.flow.configuration.archive.max.time={{.Values.properties.flowArchiveMaxTime}}\r\nnifi.flow.configuration.archive.max.storage={{.Values.properties.flowArchiveMaxStorage}}\r\nnifi.flow.configuration.archive.max.count=\r\nnifi.flowcontroller.autoResumeState=true\r\nnifi.flowcontroller.graceful.shutdown.period=10
  sec\r\nnifi.flowservice.writedelay.interval=500 ms\r\nnifi.administrative.yield.duration=30
  sec\r\n# If a component has no work to do (is \"bored\"), how long should we wait
  before checking again for work?\r\nnifi.bored.yield.duration=10 millis\r\n\r\nnifi.authorizer.configuration.file=./conf/authorizers.xml\r\nnifi.login.identity.provider.configuration.file=./conf/login-identity-providers.xml\r\nnifi.templates.directory=../data/templates\r\nnifi.ui.banner.text=\r\nnifi.ui.autorefresh.interval=30
  sec\r\nnifi.nar.library.directory=./lib\r\nnifi.nar.library.directory.custom={{.Values.properties.customLibPath}}\r\nnifi.nar.library.autoload.directory=./extensions\r\nnifi.nar.working.directory=./work/nar/\r\nnifi.documentation.working.directory=./work/docs/components\r\n\r\n####################\r\n#
  State Management #\r\n####################\r\nnifi.state.management.configuration.file=./conf/state-management.xml\r\n#
  The ID of the local state provider\r\nnifi.state.management.provider.local=local-provider\r\n#
  The ID of the cluster-wide state provider. This will be ignored if NiFi is not clustered
  but must be populated if running in a cluster.\r\nnifi.state.management.provider.cluster=zk-provider\r\n#
  Specifies whether or not this instance of NiFi should run an embedded ZooKeeper
  server\r\nnifi.state.management.embedded.zookeeper.start=false\r\n# Properties file
  that provides the ZooKeeper properties to use if <nifi.state.management.embedded.zookeeper.start>
  is set to true\r\nnifi.state.management.embedded.zookeeper.properties=./conf/zookeeper.properties\r\n\r\n\r\n#
  H2 Settings\r\nnifi.database.directory=../data/database_repository\r\nnifi.h2.url.append=;LOCK_TIMEOUT=25000;WRITE_DELAY=0;AUTO_SERVER=FALSE\r\n\r\n#
  FlowFile Repository\r\nnifi.flowfile.repository.implementation=org.apache.nifi.controller.repository.WriteAheadFlowFileRepository\r\nnifi.flowfile.repository.directory=../flowfile_repository\r\nnifi.flowfile.repository.partitions=256\r\nnifi.flowfile.repository.checkpoint.interval=2
  mins\r\nnifi.flowfile.repository.always.sync=false\r\n\r\nnifi.swap.manager.implementation=org.apache.nifi.controller.FileSystemSwapManager\r\nnifi.queue.swap.threshold=20000\r\nnifi.swap.in.period=5
  sec\r\nnifi.swap.in.threads=1\r\nnifi.swap.out.period=5 sec\r\nnifi.swap.out.threads=4\r\n\r\n#
  Content Repository\r\nnifi.content.repository.implementation=org.apache.nifi.controller.repository.FileSystemRepository\r\nnifi.content.claim.max.appendable.size=1
  MB\r\nnifi.content.claim.max.flow.files=100\r\nnifi.content.repository.directory.default=../content_repository\r\nnifi.content.repository.archive.max.retention.period={{.Values.properties.archiveMaxRetentionPeriod}}\r\nnifi.content.repository.archive.max.usage.percentage={{.Values.properties.archiveMaxUsagePercentage}}\r\nnifi.content.repository.archive.enabled=true\r\nnifi.content.repository.always.sync=false\r\nnifi.content.viewer.url=/nifi-content-viewer/\r\n\r\n#
  Provenance Repository Properties\r\nnifi.provenance.repository.implementation=org.apache.nifi.provenance.WriteAheadProvenanceRepository\r\nnifi.provenance.repository.debug.frequency=1000000\r\nnifi.provenance.repository.encryption.key.provider.implementation=\r\nnifi.provenance.repository.encryption.key.provider.location=\r\nnifi.provenance.repository.encryption.key.id=\r\nnifi.provenance.repository.encryption.key=\r\n\r\n#
  Persistent Provenance Repository Properties\r\nnifi.provenance.repository.directory.default=../provenance_repository\r\nnifi.provenance.repository.max.storage.time={{.Values.properties.provenanceMaxStorageTime}}\r\nnifi.provenance.repository.max.storage.size={{.Values.properties.provenanceStorage}}\r\nnifi.provenance.repository.rollover.time=30
  secs\r\nnifi.provenance.repository.rollover.size=100 MB\r\nnifi.provenance.repository.query.threads=2\r\nnifi.provenance.repository.index.threads=2\r\nnifi.provenance.repository.compress.on.rollover=true\r\nnifi.provenance.repository.always.sync=false\r\nnifi.provenance.repository.journal.count=16\r\n#
  Comma-separated list of fields. Fields that are not indexed will not be searchable.
  Valid fields are:\r\n# EventType, FlowFileUUID, Filename, TransitURI, ProcessorID,
  AlternateIdentifierURI, Relationship, Details\r\nnifi.provenance.repository.indexed.fields=EventType,
  FlowFileUUID, Filename, ProcessorID, Relationship\r\n# FlowFile Attributes that
  should be indexed and made searchable.  Some examples to consider are filename,
  uuid, mime.type\r\nnifi.provenance.repository.indexed.attributes=\r\n# Large values
  for the shard size will result in more Java heap usage when searching the Provenance
  Repository\r\n# but should provide better performance\r\nnifi.provenance.repository.index.shard.size=500
  MB\r\n# Indicates the maximum length that a FlowFile attribute can be when retrieving
  a Provenance Event from\r\n# the repository. If the length of any attribute exceeds
  this value, it will be truncated when the event is retrieved.\r\nnifi.provenance.repository.max.attribute.length=65536\r\n\r\n#
